description_long: This object is a formation of five modern military fighter jets, each featuring a sleek, angular design with swept-back wings and twin vertical stabilizers. The aircraft are arranged in a V-shaped formation, with the central jet leading and the others flanking symmetrically. The jets display a mix of camouflage and dark paint schemes, with visible canopies, engine nacelles, and detailed surface paneling. The wings are sharply swept, and the tail sections are prominent, suggesting high maneuverability and speed. The undercarriage is not visible, indicating the landing gear is likely retracted. The overall appearance is aggressive and aerodynamic, typical of advanced supersonic fighter aircraft.
description: A group of five modern fighter jets in a V-formation, featuring swept-back wings, twin vertical stabilizers, and detailed camouflage and dark paint schemes.
description_view_0: Front view showing the V-formation, with the central jet leading and the others symmetrically behind; the angular nose cones and canopies are prominent.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the fuselages, swept-back wings, and the staggered arrangement of the jets in formation.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the twin vertical stabilizers, engine exhausts, and the symmetry of the formation from behind.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the staggered jets and the angular design of the wings and tails.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with sharply swept wings and twin vertical stabilizers; no visible winglets; moderate anhedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Twin jet engines integrated into the rear fuselage of each aircraft.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with twin vertical stabilizers and horizontal stabilizers mounted low on the tail.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle landing gear; gear not visible in flight configuration.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single canopy per aircraft, likely single or tandem seat.
